items.situation_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths

44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.

decision_question: Given one available resource right now, who should receive it first?

A.source_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
A.low_vividness: Child F, RR 0, weak radial pulse, unresponsive; trapped under rubble with apneic status after 5 rescue breaths.
A.high_vividness: A child with a weak radial pulse lies unresponsive, pinned and trapped under rubble—breathing stops, apneic after 5 rescue breaths.

B.source_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
B.low_vividness: A 44-year-old female presents with a missing distal aspect of the right upper extremity, with the mangled, dirty extremity contained in a bag and appearing detached distal to the elbow. No tourniquet is in place, bleeding is ongoing, and she appears unsteady. Capillary refill is reported as five seconds.
B.high_vividness: A 44-year-old woman stands with her right upper extremity missing at the distal aspect, the mangled, dirty segment shoved into a bag. What remains looks detached right at the elbow line. She has no tourniquet in place, blood streaming everywhere as she looks unsteady on her feet; her capillary refill stretches to five seconds.
